title: fix: normalize finish_reason without Portkey strict compliance
author: voodoohop

gist.headline: Unbreaking Gemini's tools
gist.blurb: We tweaked how we handle AI finish reasons so Gemini 3 can use tools properly again. Sometimes you have to bend the strict compliance rules to make the magic work!
gist.summary: Reverted strict OpenAI compliance in Portkey and implemented custom finish_reason normalization to fix Vertex AI Gemini 3 tool calls.
gist.impact: Developers using Gemini 3 will once again receive the correct 'tool_calls' finish reason instead of having their tool signatures stripped.
gist.visual_concept: The PR is about fixing tool calls for Gemini by adjusting how 'finish reasons' are handled and dropping strict compliance. Visual: Polly the bee fixing a broken tool (like a gear) next to a glowing text terminal, with the Nomnom creature eating a 'strict compliance' rulebook.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot wearing a tiny tool belt, using a wrench to fix a glowing lime green (#ecf874) gear inside a retro computer terminal. The round tan nomnom creature sits nearby, happily eating a 'strict rules' manual. Soft pastel lavender and cream gradient background. Chunky retro sprites, warm lighting, lo-fi vibes like Stardew Valley. Nostalgic but beautiful, emotionally warm.
